mission.title: Install and Configure Submariner on Kubernetes
mission.description: Submariner is a networking component designed to interconnect Pods and Services across multiple Kubernetes clusters, enabling seamless communication. Installing Submariner allows you to create secure and efficient networking between your clusters, regardless of the underlying network plugins.

steps.title: Create Submariner Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for Submariner to operate in by running the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace submariner-operator
```

steps.title: Deploy Submariner Operator
steps.description:
Apply the Submariner Operator manifest to your cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/submariner-io/submariner/v0.13.0/deploy/operator.yaml -n submariner-operator
```

steps.title: Deploy Submariner Gateway
steps.description:
Apply the Submariner Gateway manifest to your cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/submariner-io/submariner/v0.13.0/deploy/gateway.yaml -n submariner-operator
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check that the Submariner pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner-operator
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Submariner will show all pods in the 'submariner' namespace as running. Additionally, the validation command should confirm that the setup is correct and functioning as expected.

uninstall.title: Remove Submariner
uninstall.description:
To uninstall Submariner, delete the namespace and all resources within it:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace submariner-operator
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Submariner
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Submariner, first delete the existing resources and then apply the new version manifests:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/submariner-io/submariner/v0.13.0/deploy/operator.yaml -n submariner-operator
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/submariner-io/submariner/v0.14.0/deploy/operator.yaml -n submariner-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Submariner pods are not starting, check the events in the namespace:
```bash
kubectl get events -n submariner-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Network Connectivity Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you experience connectivity issues between clusters, ensure that the required ports are open and that the network policies allow traffic between the clusters.

troubleshooting.title: Check Logs for Errors
troubleshooting.description:
To diagnose issues, check the logs of the Submariner pods:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n submariner-operator
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
